name: Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos
description: Hike a difficult 13.5-mile circular route from Torrijos to explore the historical ruins of Barcience and Caudilla castles in Toledo.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Torrijos are primarily characterized by the flat to gently rolling plains of the Toledo province, often featuring agricultural landscapes and historical towns. The region's terrain includes riverine paths along the Tagus (Tajo) and open countryside connecting various villages and historical sites. Elevation changes are generally modest, making many routes accessible for a range of abilities. Best hiking trails around Torrijos The most popular hiking route is Route of the Three Villas, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects historical villas and offers insights into the local heritage. Another top favourite among local hikers is Albarreal de Tajo from Burujón by the Camino Natural de Tajo (GR 113) — circular by the Monumento Natural Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.7 km) path. This route follows the Camino Natural de Tajo and explores the natural geological formations of the Barrancas de Castrejón y Calaña. Local hikers also love the Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical castles, often completed in about 5 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Torrijos is defined by open agricultural fields, historical landmarks, and river paths along the Tagus. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Torrijos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrijos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Torrijos?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes around Torrijos, offering a variety of experiences. This includes 11 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes near Torrijos?
A: Yes, Torrijos offers 11 easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Huecas loop from Fuensalida&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.8 miles (9.4 km) long and takes around 2 hours 25 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in the Torrijos area?
A: Hikes around Torrijos vary in duration depending on their length and difficulty. Easy routes can take around 2-3 hours, while moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018324376/route-of-the-three-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Three Villas&lt;/a&gt; typically take about 3 hours 13 minutes. More difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343933484/castillos-de-barcience-y-caudilla-circular-desde-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Torrijos?
A: Yes, many routes in the Torrijos area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343933484/castillos-de-barcience-y-caudilla-circular-desde-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a loop through rural landscapes and past historical castles.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on hikes around Torrijos?
A: Hiking around Torrijos is characterized by open agricultural fields, historical landmarks, and river paths along the Tagus (Tajo). You'll encounter flat to gently rolling plains, connecting various villages and historical sites, with modest elevation changes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1343933484/castillos-de-barcience-y-caudilla-circular-desde-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past historical castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcience Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5451372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijos Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España and Monument to the Cross&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Torrijos?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Torrijos are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Torrijos?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes exploring natural geological formations like the Barrancas de Burujón, there is a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/614475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking area at Las Barrancas de Burujón&lt;/a&gt;. For other routes starting from towns, street parking or public car parks in the villages are usually available.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torrijos?
A: The trails in Torrijos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 16 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's blend of open agricultural fields, historical landmarks, and accessible river paths along the Tagus, making for varied and interesting walks.

Q: Are there any less crowded trails for a more peaceful hiking experience?
A: While specific crowd levels can vary, many of the routes traversing the open countryside and connecting smaller villages tend to be less frequented than more popular, well-known trails. Exploring some of the 11 easy routes or those slightly further from the main town center can offer a more peaceful experience.

Q: Can I reach hiking spots around Torrijos using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads outside of Torrijos town are generally limited. While Torrijos itself is accessible by bus or train from larger cities, reaching the starting points of many rural hiking routes often requires personal transportation. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby villages if you plan to rely on public transport.
tips:
Torrijos gives its name to its region in the plain between the Tagus and Alberche rivers. It is the natural center of trade in goods and services in this area of Toledo. With its population of almost 13,000 inhabitants, it has all the services of a small city and has direct communications by train and bus with the main cities of Toledo and Extremadura.

Its monuments, such as the palace of Pedro I or its collegiate church, testify to its historical importance since the Middle Ages.

We can tour its large courtyard, and the almost square plan that it presents, indicating by its size and the time in which it was erected, that it served as a residence rather than a defensive fort. Two interesting cubes are indications of the barrier and the moat that protected the entrance door to the enclosure, and of which there are no remains. Three towers, two circular, another square, and the homage, make up the rest of the enclosure.

Juan de Silva, the first Count of Cifuentes, began the construction of the fortress in the 14th century, which would be completed by his grandson, with the same name. It was endowed with artillery and garrison in the 16th century.

Although the town of Caudilla is uninhabited, the ruins of the church remain there and, even more striking, those of the castle of Rivadeneyra, which was built in the 15th century and which, curiously, after centuries of wars and harsh weather, still stands the figure of Christ at the top of its tower that makes it a very curious and striking site for many, especially for photography lovers who seek their best shot at night under a starry sky.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla — circular desde Torrijos trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 21.7 kilometers (13.5 miles) long and typically takes around 5 hours and 40 minutes to complete. This duration is for moving time and does not include stops for exploring the castles or enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hiking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied but generally characterized by the plains of Toledo. You'll encounter largely flat sections with extensive steppe habitats, cereal crops, and areas of Mediterranean forest. The path to Barcience Castle is a dirt track winding through a small pine forest, and the castle itself is on a hill. Around Caudilla's castle, you'll find an extensive high plateau with wheat fields. While much of it is 'practically flat', expect dirt tracks and potentially uneven surfaces, especially within the castle ruins.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Castillos de Barcience y Caudilla route?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the two historic castles. You'll explore the exterior of the 15th-century Barcience Castle, known for its prominent keep and carved lion emblem. Further along, you'll discover the picturesque ruins of Caudilla's castle, set within an abandoned village. The route also starts and ends near the Plaza de España and Monument to the Cross in Torrijos, and offers panoramic views of the surrounding Toledo countryside.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. Given the rural nature of the route and the presence of agricultural land, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or cultivated fields, and to ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead in Torrijos?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Torrijos. You can typically find parking in or around the town center, close to the Plaza de España, which serves as a convenient starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to hike this trail. Access to the exterior of Barcience Castle is usually free, and there are no entrance fees for the trail itself or the ruins of Caudilla Castle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this trail are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, making the long distance more comfortable. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers pleasant colors and cooler air. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and varied terrain, wear comfortable and appropriate footwear, such as hiking boots or sturdy walking shoes, especially for the dirt tracks and uneven surfaces around the castles. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly hike?
faqs.answer: While the terrain itself is not overly challenging in terms of elevation, the 21.7 km (13.5 miles) distance makes it a difficult and long hike, which might be too strenuous for very young children. It could be suitable for older children or teenagers who are accustomed to long-distance walking.

faqs.question: Are there any unique historical connections on this trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Both castles are steeped in history. Barcience Castle was a stately residence of the influential Silva family, while Caudilla Castle is linked to the Rivadeneyra family and events involving King Enrique IV. Additionally, sections of the route near Barcience may coincide with the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, adding another layer of historical and cultural interest to your journey.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for visiting the castles on this route?
faqs.answer: For a truly atmospheric experience, consider timing your visit to Caudilla Castle for late afternoon or sunset. Its location in an abandoned village, combined with minimal light pollution, makes it strikingly picturesque and a popular spot for photography as the light fades.

waypoints.name: Plaza de España and Monument to the Cross, Torrijos
View of Caudilla's church and castle
